sources.label: Investing.com - Anritsu shares rise 5.8% on stronger fiscal 2026 guidance (late-April event, pre-window)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved Anritsu down for the +87% YTD rerating; no new fundamental evidence has emerged since June 6 and today's +0.28% drift changes nothing, so hold at rank 57 with mild residual-alpha decay.
verdicts.bull: FY2026 guidance (OP Y20B vs Y17.8B consensus) and a backlog up 40% confirm the 800G/1.6T optical-transceiver and PCIe 7.0 test cycle is real, and at ~$3.7B market cap Anritsu remains a small, picks-and-shovels way to play AI interconnect validation.
verdicts.neutral: The design-validation thesis is intact but already priced for strong execution; with no fresh design wins or orders disclosed since June 6, the stock is in digestion mode after the guidance-driven rerating.
verdicts.bear: An 87% YTD run on a cyclical test-and-measurement name leaves little hidden alpha; mobile/5G softness still drags the core, and any slip in transceiver test demand or T&M margin would unwind the rerating quickly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY2026 results and order intake (July) confirming transceiver-test momentum against the 24% T&M growth assumption.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/rerating risk: guidance is now the bar, so any moderation in AI datacenter test orders or hyperscaler capex digestion hits a stock priced for acceleration.
